interpretation: Fleet sizes below N=6 yield fewer than 1 expected repeat attack signature over a 30-day window, given 10,000 possible signatures, 100 calls/agent/day, and 2.0 attacks/1000 agent-days. Vaccination relies on cross-agent signature reuse; when reuse is statistically absent, immunization cache cannot be populated or queried against a second occurrence within the operational horizon.
verdict: Below N=6 fleet size, vaccination is theater — attacks are too unique to repeat within 30 days. Above N=6, vaccination provides measurable protection (expected repeats >= 1 per month).
